---
title: "02 — Clean & filter articles"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
PROJECT_ROOT <- Sys.getenv("CAPLAN_ROOT")
if (PROJECT_ROOT == "") PROJECT_ROOT <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("_config.R"))
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = PROJECT_ROOT)
source(file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "_config.R"))
source(file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "R", "helpers.R"))
library(arrow); library(data.table)
```

Cleans the raw per-year Parquet into a partitioned dataset `data_parquet/articles_cleaned/`
(one part per year). Steps: ensure a stable `article_id`, normalize whitespace, compute an
OCR-quality proxy, drop unusably short/noisy articles, and stamp `year`.

## Inspect the raw schema first

```{r schema}
raw_files <- list.files(DATA_PARQUET, pattern = "^articles_\\d{4}\\.parquet$",
                        full.names = TRUE)
stopifnot(length(raw_files) > 0)
print(names(read_parquet(raw_files[1], as_data_frame = FALSE)$schema))
# American Stories typically exposes: article_id, newspaper_name, edition, date,
# page, headline, byline, article (text), and an lccn-bearing id. Adjust the
# column names below if your snapshot differs.
TEXT_COL <- "article"      # main body text
ID_COL   <- "article_id"   # stable id (created from a hash if absent)
```

## Clean, filter, partition

```{r clean}
cleaned_dir <- file.path(DATA_PARQUET, "articles_cleaned")
dir.create(cleaned_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Tunable filters (record chosen values in docs/PROJECT_PLAN.md if changed)
MIN_WORDS   <- 25      # drop fragments
MAX_NONALPHA <- 0.40   # drop badly OCR'd scans

for (rf in raw_files) {
  yr <- as.integer(gsub(".*articles_(\\d{4})\\.parquet", "\\1", rf))
  outdir  <- file.path(cleaned_dir, paste0("year=", yr))
  outpath <- file.path(outdir, "part-0.parquet")
  if (file.exists(outpath)) { message("Year ", yr, " already cleaned, skip"); next }

  dt <- as.data.table(read_parquet(rf))
  setnames(dt, TEXT_COL, "article", skip_absent = TRUE)

  # stable id
  if (!ID_COL %in% names(dt)) {
    dt[, article_id := paste0(yr, "_", .I)]
  } else if (ID_COL != "article_id") {
    setnames(dt, ID_COL, "article_id")
  }

  # normalize whitespace; compute quality metrics
  dt[, article := stringi::stri_replace_all_regex(article, "\\s+", " ")]
  dt[, article := trimws(article)]
  dt[, word_count   := count_words(article)]
  dt[, ocr_nonalpha := share_nonalpha(article)]
  dt[, year := yr]

  before <- nrow(dt)
  dt <- dt[word_count >= MIN_WORDS & (is.na(ocr_nonalpha) | ocr_nonalpha <= MAX_NONALPHA)]
  # exact-duplicate text drop (syndicated boilerplate handled separately later)
  dt <- unique(dt, by = "article")

  dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  write_parquet(dt, outpath)
  message(sprintf("Year %d: %d -> %d articles kept (%.1f%%)",
                  yr, before, nrow(dt), 100 * nrow(dt) / before))
  rm(dt); gc()
}
```

## Summary

```{r summary}
ds <- open_dataset(cleaned_dir)
library(dplyr)
ds |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(n = n(), med_words = median(word_count, na.rm = TRUE),
            med_nonalpha = median(ocr_nonalpha, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  collect() |> arrange(year) |> print(n = 100)
```
